stem: The function p(x) = 320,000(1.15)x gives a company's predicted annual revenue, in dollars, x years after the company began selling cookware online, where 0 < x ≤ 10. What is the best interpretation of the statement “p(6) is approximately equal to 740,160” in this context?

answerOptions.content: When the company's predicted annual revenue is approximately 740,160 dollars, it is 6 times the predicted annual revenue for the previous year.
6 years after the company began selling cookware online, its predicted annual revenue is approximately 740,160 dollars.
6 years after the company began selling cookware online, its predicted annual revenue will have increased by a total of approximately 740,160 dollars.
When the company's predicted annual revenue is approximately 740,160 dollars, it is 6% greater than the predicted annual revenue for the previous year.

rationale:
Choice B is correct. The function p(x) gives the predicted annual revenue in dollars x years after the company began selling cookware online. So p(6) ≈ 740,160 means that 6 years after the company began selling cookware online, the predicted annual revenue is approximately 740,160 dollars.

Choices A, C, and D misinterpret the meaning of p(6) by confusing the value of the function with a change or a multiplicative comparison.
